rationale: Both correctly diagnose the crash as `<img_ratio_33..36>` lookups returning None on the base tokenizer, correctly attribute it to the two-stage MoE config forcing AR init, and cite the same working DiT-config workaround from the thread. Y goes further by supplying concrete guarded-init code and an explicit fail-fast error message (matching the thread's two-part 'Fix Docs / Add a Guard' proposal more completely), and by correctly treating the original bug as resolved/closeable while the hardening work moves to a new issue — matching Gaohan123's actual disposition — whereas X marks the issue 'keep-open', a weaker match to the thread's resolution. Both are comparably grounded in the same file/line citations, but Y cites additional config-header evidence, giving it a slight edge in grounding and completeness.
